fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness on the property could be achieved by ensuring clear and wide access via the front driveway, which serves as the single entry point. Creating designated staging areas near the entrance would support efficient deployment of emergency resources. Improving the sidewalk network by confirming well-marked and unobstructed pathways may facilitate responder movement and navigation around the property. Implementing strategic landscaping that avoids obstructing sightlines around key areas of the property could enhance visibility for responders. Providing additional access or egress routes, if possible, near the rear of the property might further assist in streamlining operations and allowing emergency personnel to quickly reach all areas of the site.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property can be achieved by managing the vegetation adjacent to the structure and promoting separation between existing vegetation and the building. Prioritizing the reduction of fuel continuity along the vegetated perimeter could decrease the potential for fire spread towards the structure. The presence of an open terrain and an open fuel break around the property should be maintained and possibly expanded to further mitigate fuel continuity, serving as effective barriers to wildfire progression. Addressing the limited side access by ensuring clear and unobstructed routes can facilitate firefighter access and suppression efforts. Additionally, preserving surrounding open spaces free of dense vegetation will support long-term resilience against wildfires, improving overall property preparedness.
